gene_description: UDP-glucuronosyltransferase Family 1 Member A1 — catalyzes the glucuronidation and elimination of bilirubin and many drugs. The rs8175347 TA repeat polymorphism in the TATA box promoter regulates enzyme expression and activity.

source.title: Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium (CPIC) Guideline for UGT1A1 and Irinotecan Prescribing
source.finding: UGT1A1*28 (TA7 repeats from rs8175347) reduces enzyme activity to approximately 30% of normal, increasing risk of severe irinotecan toxicity (neutropenia, diarrhea) and bilirubin elevation with atazanavir.

TA6/TA6.effect: Wild-type genotype with normal UGT1A1 expression and glucuronidation capacity. Normal bilirubin metabolism and drug elimination. Normal bilirubin levels (typically <1.2 mg/dL).
TA6/TA6.recommendation: Standard irinotecan dosing per protocol. Standard atazanavir dosing appropriate. No dose adjustments needed. Routine monitoring of liver function and bilirubin.

TA6/TA7.effect: Heterozygous carriers with intermediate UGT1A1 expression (approximately 70% of normal activity). Slightly elevated bilirubin levels (often 1.2-3.0 mg/dL without clinical jaundice). Moderate risk for irinotecan dose-limiting toxicity (severe diarrhea, neutropenia).
TA6/TA7.recommendation: Irinotecan: Use standard dosing but with close toxicity monitoring. Consider 10-15% dose reduction if dose-limiting toxicity observed. Atazanavir: Standard dosing, monitor for hyperbilirubinemia and jaundice. If bilirubin elevates, may require alternative protease inhibitor.

TA7/TA7.effect: Homozygous TA7 carriers (UGT1A1*28/*28) with significantly reduced enzyme activity (approximately 30% of normal). Chronic unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia (typically 2-5 mg/dL) without hemolysis or hepatic dysfunction. High risk for severe irinotecan toxicity and atazanavir-related hyperbilirubinemia with jaundice.
TA7/TA7.recommendation: Irinotecan: FDA recommends 25-30% dose reduction from standard protocol starting dose. Monitor CBC and diarrhea closely during first cycle. Atazanavir: Use with caution or consider alternative protease inhibitor. If used, baseline and regular bilirubin monitoring mandatory. Patient counseling on expected benign mild jaundice. Avoid concurrent use with irinotecan.
